process_section.capital_use_details: Acquire regional competitors in Southeast Asia to consolidate trade receivables financing platform. Target 3 acquisitions over 24 months to create pan-APAC player.
process_section.irr_assumptions: 4-year hold, 3.5x revenue multiple exit (trading at 3x now), 40% annual organic growth + bolt-on accretion, $20M check gets 18% equity stake
process_section.value_driver_details: Current platform has 15% quarterly growth. We acquire 2-3 smaller regional competitors at 1.5x revenue. Post-consolidation: (1) cross-sell to 5x customer base, (2) reduce CAC by 40% via integrated platform, (3) expand from $15M to $80M revenue by exit, (4) exit at 3.5x revenue vs. current 3x given scale and growth profile.

legal_section.governance_details: One board seat + quarterly financial reporting. Veto rights on: capital raises >$2M, debt >$5M, new customer commitments >$10M, management changes (CRO, CFO, CTO), asset sales.

compliance_section.aml_kyc_notes: All beneficial owners (3) and board members (5) completed third-party KYC verification via Compliance Labs on 2026-04-20. All clear.
compliance_section.esg_notes: SOC 2 Type II certified (2025). Female CEO and CFO. Diversity: 42% women in workforce, 28% in tech. No labor violations. Board has 2 independent directors. Anti-bribery training mandatory.
compliance_section.reputational_notes: No major controversies. One minor employment dispute settled in 2024 (unrelated to current management). Clean media record. No litigation threats.

ceo.name: Mei-Li Wong
ceo.background: Ex-DBS Bank (fintech lead), 12 years trade finance experience, MBA from NUS

key_executives.name: Rajesh Kumar
key_executives.background: Ex-Stripe (payment systems), 10 years, Singapore Tech Award winner

notes: Strong founding team with deep fintech and trade finance expertise. Rapid growth (40% YoY) and clear path to profitability. Trade receivables is $2.5T global market with <3% digital penetration in APAC. Company is addressing the wedge use case (small/medium supplier financing) where manual processes cost institutional investors $500B+ in lost productivity annually. Strategic buyer interest from regional banks.
